title: Tabebuia elliptica
intro: Tabebuia elliptica, also known as the Pink Trumpet Tree, is a tropical flowering plant native to Central and South America, characterized by its vibrant pink flowers and elliptical leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, offering potential health benefits in treating various conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Tabebuia elliptica include naphthoquinones, such as lapachol and its derivatives, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Tabebuia elliptica include teas, tinctures, and extracts, often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms associated with infections.
taxonomy: Tabebuia elliptica is a species within the family Bignoniaceae. This family is part of the larger order Lamiales, which is a group of flowering plants. The Bignoniaceae family is known for its diverse range of flowering species, many of which have showy and fragrant blooms. The classification of Tabebuia elliptica places it within the subfamily Bignonioideae. By being part of this subfamily, Tabebuia elliptica shares characteristics with other flowering plants that have similar growth habits and flower structures.
uses: Tabebuia elliptica, also known as the pink poui, is a plant with reported medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine.
